ground_truth_source: NAS /Volumes/transit (Rush, loaded by evaluate_detection.py default paths)

baseline.notes: Run 43 ceiling-break baseline: F1 0.409, P 0.422, R 0.397, TP 62. Run 44 (7 frames) regressed to 0.369 and Run 45 (7 frames cross-game on sporting_ac) improved only marginally with coarse-firing rate unchanged, so density was rejected as the primary lever. Reverted to 5 frames. Run 46 ships prompt surgery: the templated 3-check 'throw-in?/catch?/corner? -> open play' default path is replaced with a broader stoppage-binary that asks whether play is continuous or any stoppage signal is visible anywhere (ball stationary; player holding ball; set-piece formation; touchline/corner walk; ref raised arm; OOB; center-circle reset).

targets.notes: Prompt restructure — Rush F1 can tolerate slight precision drop if recall rebounds, but F1 must not regress below Run 43's 0.409. Real prize is cross-game: if Rush holds, immediately run this prompt on sporting_ac (Run 47) to see if coarse-firing rate moves off 3.1% on non-Rush footage. FP inflation on Rush within tolerance (<=15 above baseline) is acceptable; recall jump on cross-game is the goal.

prompt_default_path.old: BEFORE you conclude open play, CHECK THROW-IN/CATCH/CORNER... only return none if ALL THREE checks are clearly negative
prompt_default_path.new: BEFORE concluding open play, answer ONE question: is play CONTINUOUS or is ANY stoppage/restart visible in any frame? (primitive signals: ball stationary, player holding ball, set-piece formation, touchline walk, ref raised arm, OOB, center-reset)
prompt_default_path.reason: The 3-check template was running reflexively on cross-game footage where those specific cues are harder to resolve. Switching to a broader stoppage-binary forces the VLM to engage with whole-frame play state rather than three framing-dependent pose cues. Free_kick_shot REQUIRES clause preserved (Run 43's FP 30->7 win).

notes: Run #46 — prompt surgery only (frames reverted to 5). Commit 3c7f555. Pre-flight verification pending on the new job.
